assertions_verified.evidence: registerServiceWorker() calls navigator.serviceWorker.register('./sw.js', { scope: './' }) — relative URL; resolved scope is the entrypoint's directory.
manifest.json exists with display:'standalone'; referenced from index.html via <link rel='manifest' href='./manifest.json'> (set up during integration).

Notification.requestPermission() is invoked only inside enableNotifications(); section-4/section-5 only call enableNotifications() from button onclick handlers. Static scan: no boot-time or timer-deferred requestPermission calls.
After requestPermission grants, enableNotifications() calls pushManager.subscribe(...) and persists via savePushSubscription(_currentGameId, record) — endpoint, keys.p256dh, keys.auth all populated.

sendTurnNotification signs a VAPID JWT (ES256, P-256) and POSTs to sub.endpoint with Authorization: vapid t=<jwt>, k=<pub>. Service worker push handler renders a notification via self.registration.showNotification. Production-fidelity verification deferred to section-7 with real Firebase project.

platformSupportsPush() returns false on unsupported platforms; notificationStatus returns {permission, subscribed, platform_supports_push, ios_requires_home_screen_install}; enableNotifications returns {granted:false, reason:'unsupported'|'denied'|'requires_pwa_install'} without throwing. Section-4 reads notificationStatus and renders fallback UI when push is unavailable.
